title: Delicate Slovakian Perilla Leaf Dumplings
description: A light and buttery Slovakian dish featuring perilla leaf, perfect for a snack or lunch, this jain friendly recipe is wrapped in a banana leaf vessel
instructions: Start by preparing the perilla leaves by washing and drying them thoroughly.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, and black pepper.
Gradually add the water to the flour mixture and knead until a smooth dough forms.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a small egg.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a perilla leaf in the center of each dough circle.
Place a tablespoon of unsalted butter and a sprinkle of feta cheese on top of the perilla leaf.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Wrap each dumpling in a banana leaf and steam for 10 minutes.
Serve the dumplings hot with a side of melted butter for dipping.
